parsed_review.summary: CP00-735 is a descriptor-only Korean Legal Depth fixture/contract bridge pack (RP24, risk C, 150 units, RP24.P04.M06.S05-RP24.P05.M03.S06) and PASSES read-only review. Identity and scope match the baseline across manifest, registry binding (KOREAN_LEGAL_CP735_PACK_BINDING), contract current_pack/latest_pack, and review baseline; deliverable distribution {ui:39,claude_review:12,security_audit:14,implementation:39,fixture:30,test:10,hermes_evidence:6} and micro-title distribution both sum to 150 and match the prompt exactly. Implementation is present and consistent with the 13 predecessor packs: registry CP735 requirements/exports, service.js factories (case set + descriptor + hermes/claude packets + closeout handoff) chained from CP734, validators.js coverage+descriptor validators, and index.js re-exports. The validate-rp24-korean-legal-depth-contract.mjs script makes CP735 the latest entry, asserts empty coverage/descriptor errors, emits_runtime_receipt=false, claude read_only=true, and handoff continuity, then regenerates the contract; the contract has current_pack/latest_pack=CP00-735 and zero "valid":false entries. model.test.js adds three CP735 tests (binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, contract-projection-latest with validation.valid===true). command-evidence.json reports all ten required validation commands at exit 0 (plus node --check and build); provenance note: I could not re-run these (Bash disallowed), but direct source inspection of the validators, contract, and tests corroborates the reported PASS. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are intact: NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and ROW_EXTRAS keep writes_product_state, database/object-storage writes, external runtime, and all HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate-document/Korean-clause-final-authority surfaces false; a secret/PII scan of the changed package was clean; descriptors carry only boolean flags and IDs (fixture_payload_included=false). Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer (claude_packet.read_only=true, authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true), with allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob and the CP178+ forbidden-evidence list (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose). Hermes packets are deterministic control-plane review/receipt artifacts only (receipt_shape descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write, emits_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. The pack does not overclaim: production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false are accurate, and production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label rather than a runtime or enterprise-trust claim. Per the CP-closeout-only interpretation of blocks_goal_closeout, Claude not being the final human/enterprise-trust approver does not block this pack's closeout. The only observation is informational/non-blocking (P3): the pack-level claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are in their expected pre-review PENDING state and must be finalized by the orchestrator from this review receipt before commit.

findings.title: Pack-level review/adjudication/inspection files remain in expected pre-review PENDING state
findings.details: construction-inspection.json (verdict PENDING, pack_level_claude_review_completed=false, korean_legal_p04_p05_fixture_contract_bridge_descriptor_verified=false), adjudication.md (P0-P3 pending, production-ready-after-adjudication: no), and claude-review-result.json (status not_started, parsed_review.overall_verdict PENDING with blocks_pack_closeout=true / blocks_goal_closeout=true) are placeholders awaiting exactly one valid hardened read-only Claude review receipt. This is the correct state for a pack whose review (this run) has not yet been recorded; manifest.pack_level_claude_review.status is likewise not_started with empty valid_review_receipts. It is not a defect in the pack implementation, but the closeout gate depends on these fields being updated from this PASS receipt.
findings.recommendation: After this receipt is emitted, have the orchestrator propagate the PASS result into claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json (and manifest.pack_level_claude_review) so the closeout gate reflects the recorded review before committing CP00-735. No change required from the pack author.
